macro.intro: The macroeconomic environment is characterized by rising inflation with CPI YoY at 330.29% and a stable Fed Funds Rate at 3.64%. The 10Y TIPS Real Yield has slightly increased to 1.9%, indicating moderate inflation expectations. The 10Y-2Y Yield Spread is at 0.54%, suggesting a stable yield curve. No major economic events are scheduled today, allowing markets to focus on geopolitical developments.

setups.name: Gold Long on Geopolitical Tensions
setups.rationale: Gold is benefiting from geopolitical tensions with Iran, trading at 1985.50. The potential for a US-Iran peace deal could drive further gains. CPI YoY at 330.29% supports inflation hedging. No major economic events today to disrupt this trend.

metrics.label: CPI YoY Inflation
metrics.text: High inflation supports gold as a hedge

concerns.text: Peace deal could reduce safe-haven demand, pushing prices below 1960

concerns.label: Secondary risk
concerns.text: Unexpected Fed rate hike could strengthen USD

setups.hedge: Consider shorting EUR/USD if gold falls below 1960.

setups.name: WTI Short on Peace Deal Prospects
setups.rationale: WTI Crude Oil is trading at 72.30, down 1.20% today. The potential for a US-Iran peace deal is weighing on prices. The 10Y-2Y Yield Spread at 0.54% suggests stable economic conditions, reducing demand for oil as a risk asset.

metrics.label: Geopolitical News
metrics.text: Peace talks could further depress oil prices

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: Escalation in Iran conflict could spike prices above 74.50

setups.hedge: Consider long S&P 500 if oil rises above 74.50.

setups.name: S&P 500 Long on Risk-On Sentiment
setups.rationale: The S&P 500 is trading at 4500, up 0.30% today, driven by risk-on sentiment amid US-Iran peace deal hopes. The VIX at 18.50 indicates reduced volatility. The unemployment rate at 4.3% supports economic stability.

metrics.label: Economic event

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: Sudden geopolitical escalation could push index below 4450

setups.hedge: Consider shorting Gold if S&P 500 falls below 4450.

contrarian.body: The EUR/USD is trading at 1.1050, with potential downside as the dollar steadies near pre-war levels. The 10Y TIPS Real Yield at 1.9% supports USD strength. The lack of major economic events today allows for a focus on technical levels.
contrarian.warning: A sudden shift in US-Iran relations could strengthen the euro. Monitor geopolitical news closely for any unexpected developments.
